enum_values.name: MAIN_MODE
enum_values.description: Voltage regulator on during Deepsleep/Sleep/Low-power run mode

enum_values.name: LOW_POWER_MODE
enum_values.description: Voltage regulator in low-power mode during Deepsleep/Sleep/Low-power run mode

enum_values.name: STOP_MODE
enum_values.description: Enter Stop mode when the CPU enters deepsleep

enum_values.name: STANDBY_MODE
enum_values.description: Enter Standby mode when the CPU enters deepsleep

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clear the WUF Wakeup flag after 2 system clock cycles

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clear the SBF Standby flag

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Access to RTC, RTC Backup and RCC CSR registers disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Access to RTC, RTC Backup and RCC CSR registers enabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: VREFINT is on in low-power mode

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: VREFINT is off in low-power mode

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Low-power modes exit occurs only when VREFINT is ready

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: VREFINT start up time is ignored when exiting low-power modes

enum_values.name: V1_8
enum_values.description: 1.8 V (range 1)

enum_values.name: V1_5
enum_values.description: 1.5 V (range 2)

enum_values.name: V1_2
enum_values.description: 1.2 V (range 3)

fields.name: DS_EE_KOFF
fields.description: Deep sleep mode with Flash memory kept off

enum_values.name: NVMWakeUp
enum_values.description: NVM woken up when exiting from Deepsleep mode even if the bit RUN_PD is set

enum_values.name: NVMSleep
enum_values.description: NVM not woken up when exiting from low-power mode (if the bit RUN_PD is set)

enum_values.name: MAIN_MODE
enum_values.description: Voltage regulator in Main mode in Low-power run mode

enum_values.name: LOW_POWER_MODE
enum_values.description: Voltage regulator in low-power mode in Low-power run mode

enum_values.name: MAIN_MODE
enum_values.description: Voltage regulator in Main mode during Deepsleep mode (Stop mode)

enum_values.name: LOW_POWER_MODE
enum_values.description: Voltage regulator switches to low-power mode when the CPU enters Deepsleep mode (Stop mode)

enum_values.name: NoWakeupEvent
enum_values.description: No wakeup event occurred

enum_values.name: WakeupEvent
enum_values.description: A wakeup event was received from the WKUP pin or from the RTC alarm (Alarm A or Alarm B), RTC Tamper event, RTC TimeStamp event or RTC Wakeup)

enum_values.name: NoStandbyEvent
enum_values.description: Device has not been in Standby mode

enum_values.name: StandbyEvent
enum_values.description: Device has been in Standby mode

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: VREFINT is OFF

enum_values.name: Ready
enum_values.description: VREFINT is ready

enum_values.name: Ready
enum_values.description: Regulator is ready in the selected voltage range

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: Regulator voltage output is changing to the required VOS level

enum_values.name: Ready
enum_values.description: Regulator is ready in Main mode

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: Regulator voltage is in low-power mode

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: WKUP pin 1 is used for general purpose I/Os. An event on the WKUP pin 1 does not wakeup the device from Standby mode

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: WKUP pin 1 is used for wakeup from Standby mode and forced in input pull down configuration (rising edge on WKUP pin 1 wakes-up the system from Standby mode)

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: WKUP pin 2 is used for general purpose I/Os. An event on the WKUP pin 2 does not wakeup the device from Standby mode

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: WKUP pin 2 is used for wakeup from Standby mode and forced in input pull down configuration (rising edge on WKUP pin 2 wakes-up the system from Standby mode)

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: WKUP pin 3 is used for general purpose I/Os. An event on the WKUP pin 3 does not wakeup the device from Standby mode

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: WKUP pin 3 is used for wakeup from Standby mode and forced in input pull down configuration (rising edge on WKUP pin 3wakes-up the system from Standby mode)
